questions.question: In digital logic, what does a small circle (bubble) at the output of a logic gate symbol typically represent?
Which addressing mode calculates the effective memory address by adding the contents of a base register to a constant offset value provided in the instruction?
In the digital IC design flow, what is the primary purpose of "Formal Equivalence Checking" (FEC) tools?
options: To synthesize RTL code into gate-level netlists
To verify that two different representations of a design (e.g., RTL vs. Gate-level) are functionally identical
To perform static timing analysis on the post-layout netlist
To generate test vectors for manufacturing test
To extract parasitic resistance and capacitance from the layout

questions.question: What characteristic of Dynamic Random-Access Memory (DRAM) necessitates periodic "refresh cycles" to retain data?
options: Its reliance on flip-flops which can spontaneously invert state
The susceptibility of its magnetic domains to external interference
Its use of latches as storage elements
The charge stored in its capacitors leaks over time
Its volatile nature requires constant power to operate

questions.question: In digital CMOS circuits, which type of power consumption occurs primarily due to the charging and discharging of load capacitances during switching events?
